Title: Alive As Wind And Fire
Lyrics:
[Verse]
 I've got a wild race horse mind. No one can keep up with it. I just try to keep it together because it's never going to fit.

 Peculiar as I am, crazy as I seem, I'm alive as the wind and fire, I'm wide awake in my life's dream. 
 
 [Verse]
 One bright new idea is so shiny. I think, yeah, that's what I should do. But then it's off to another vision more beguiling, more brand new. 
 
 Am I climbing Jacob's ladder? All my visions seem so swell. Or am I just riding on a freight train? One that’s headed straight for hell. 
 
 I don't know where we’ll get to. It's not like I have any real choice. I don't know how to control it. I only hear its voice. 
 
 Chorus]
 I’ve never known what’s the answer, I just hold on for my dear life. I love the view from atop of the mountain, so I put up with the terror and the strife. 
 
 Peculiar as I am, crazy as I seem, I'm alive as the wind and fire, I'm wide awake in my life's dream. 
 
 [Verse]
 I guess I’ll ride it out. It’s taking me this far. The folks on the TV say what we should be, but is that what we really are? 
 
 Do they know where we’re really going? More than this grass fire in my mind? Do they know the right direction? Or are they all just flying blind? 
 
 [Chorus]
 I’ve never known what’s the answer, I just hold on for my dear life. I love the view from atop of the mountain, so I put up with the terror and the strife. 
 
 Peculiar as I am, crazy as I seem, I'm alive as the wind and fire, I'm wide awake in my life's dream.

Title: Coming Home
Lyrics:
[Instrumental intro]
 I’ve searched my whole life over
 To find my place in God's eternal plan
 But at the end of all my travels
 I find myself right back where I began
 
 Does that mean that all my life’s journeys 
 All my wanderings and false starts
 Were ill-conceived in a broken brain
 Or was I sent afar sometimes to touch folk's hearts
 
 As I sit here in the early evening
 Admiring the view at the end of the day
 I’m just not sure if I’ve done much good
 Given how often I stumbled along my way
 
 I look out over slowly darkening tree tops
 To the sea below that’s all aglow
 In the evening light that’s liquid amber
 In this world I’ve come to love and cherish so
 
 I’ll have to be content with hoping
 Whether I mattered little or mattered much
 That I've lived a life rich with meaning
 Because of those my heart has touched
 
 I can’t say if I did anything for most of them
 I can’t see deep within their hearts
 Surely for some I counted for little or nothing
 For though we met, soon we would depart
 
 But I can feel a welcoming spirit
 Inside of me that's always reaching out
 And I hope that when I’m remembered
 Folks will believe that's what I was all about
 
 I’ve searched my whole life over
 To find my place in God's eternal plan
 But at the end of all my travels
 I find myself right back where I began
 
 Life gave me a chance to care. for others
 To share something I know comes from above
 Though at times I may have stumbled and fallen
 I hope on balance I’ve shown others mostly love
 
 I hope on balance I’ve shown others mostly love

Title: I’m Alive As Wind And Fire
Lyrics:
[Verse]
 I've got a wild race horse mind. No one can keep up with it. I just try to keep it together because I never seem to fit. 
 
 I don't know what it's going to do. I don't know where it's going to go. It just gallops on forever. I just hang on trying to go with the flow. 
 
 [Chorus]
 I’ve never known what’s the answer, I just hold on for my dear life. I love the view from on top of a mountain, so I put up with the terror of its strife.
 
 Peculiar as I am, crazy as I seem, I'm alive as the wind and fire, and I'm wide awake in my life's dream. 
 
 [Verse]
 One bright new idea is so shiny. I think, yeah, that's what I should do. But then it's off to another vision more beguiling, even more brand new. 
 
 Am I climbing Jacob's ladder? All my visions seem so swell. Or am I just riding on a freight train? One that’s headed straight for hell. 
 
 I don't know where we’ll get to. It's not like I have any real choice. I don't know how to control it. I’m just swept up by its voice. 
 
 [Chorus]
 I’ve never known what’s the answer, I just hold on for my dear life. I love the view from on top of a mountain, so I put up with the terror of its strife.
 
 Peculiar as I am, crazy as I seem, I'm alive as the wind and fire, and I'm wide awake in my life's dream. 
 
 [Verse]
 I guess I’ll ride it out. It’s taken me this far. The folks on the TV say what we should be, but is that what we are? 
 
 Do they know where we’re really going? More than this grass fire in my mind? Do they know the right direction? Or are they all just flying blind? 
 
 [Chorus]
 I’ve never known what’s the answer, I just hold on for my dear life. I love the view from on top of a mountain, so I put up with the terror of its strife.
 
 Peculiar as I am, crazy as I seem, I'm alive as the wind and fire, and I'm wide awake in my life's dream.

Title: On The Side Of My Dreams (Country version)

 To provide me with refreshed courage 
 To continue to want to roll the dice

 Are the moans of the wounded

Title: The Cowboy Poet’s Lament
Lyrics:

 Perhaps it’s always been that way 
 I only mean to set the world on fire

 When I meet some people at first

 They are dying inside of thirst
 I always want to quench it 
 When living water springs up in me 
 My mind taps right into its spirit
 And it flows out in my poetry

 And so often it hits its mark 
 I can see when someone’s lifted 
 And I know I’ve lit a spark
 But so often they will tire 
 And their step will slow or stop 
 I can feel them pulling backwards
 They want living water but only in small drops
 Will no one ever have the courage 
 To take flight and stay with me 
 It’s our preference for the ordinary
 That roots us in our misery
 I guess this must be my fate 
 It’s a cup that will not pass 
 To always feel loneliness 
 And with love just sigh, alas
 When the spirit springs up inside of me 
 For me its always been this way
 Living water flows out in poetry 
 To set the world on fire by what it has to say
 By what it has to say

 Whose face is marred by dust and sweat and blood
 Who strives valiantly; who errs, who comes short again and again
 Who spends himself in a worthy cause
 Who at the best knows in the end the triumph of high achievement,

 at least fails while daring greatly
 So that his place shall never be with those cold and timid souls
 Who neither know victory nor defeat.”
 Yes, Teddy nailed it when he said that

Or are we all just flying blind? I’ve never known the answer, I just hold on for your life. I love the view from atop of the mountain, so I put up with the strife. Title: Before I Fell For You - Broadway
Lyrics:
I used to see myself as a cowboy 
 riding the range , yeah that’s the thing that I’d do
 Sitting tall all alone in the saddle, 
 But that’s before I fell for you 
 
 I used to sail the seas in my mind like a pirate 
 Wind at my back, days bursting bright and brand new
 Youth and lust steered my course and I followed
 But that’s before I fell for you
 
 I used to be a bronco bull rider 
 I’d match their rage, ‘cause that’s my temperament too 
 The animal in me completely took over 
 But that’s before I fell for you 
 
 I used to dance in the ring like a fighter
 There was fire in my eyes even though they were blue
 I’d conquer my foe or I’d die trying
 But that was before I fell for you
 
 Maybe all those were merely just daydreams 
 Yet each in their way finally came true
 When I surrendered all of myself so completely
 And letting you in I found my heart in you
Style Tags:
Contemporary Broadway solo for male vocal; playful, romantic, and reflective, about a man who once imagined himself as a cowboy, pirate, bull rider, and fighter before love changed him. Piano- and acoustic-guitar-led orchestration with warm strings and light percussion; gentle groove, no jazz or rock edge. Moderate tempo, storytelling and lyrical, with a clear, singable hook on “but that’s before I fell for you.” Verses feel like vivid, slightly humorous fantasies, while the final section (“Maybe all those were merely just daydreams…”) turns more earnest and heartfelt. Mostly diatonic harmony with a few tasteful Broadway-style lifts on key emotional lines; no swing feel. Arrangement builds from a simple, intimate start to a modest, uplifting climax on the realization that surrendering to love let him “find my heart in you,” ending warmly rather than as a big showstopper.
 
 Sources

Title: Claire’s Emotional Multitool
Lyrics:
I got a Christmas gift this year 
 It’s one that’s really cool 
 I keep it with me on my belt 
 It’s my girl'’s emotional multitool
 
 It’s got all kinds of little gadgets
 That fold together into one
 Some good for when you’re in trauma
 Some are just plain good old fun
 
 There’s the one that prys you open 
 To let the sunshine come streaming in 
 To clean up that infection 
 That got buried deep within 
 
 There’s the one you use to tickle 
 Yourself with a silly thought 
 That makes you forget to worry 
 About the nonsense you’ve been taught 
 
 There’s the one that’s hard to get to 
 In the center of the set 
 That makes you reconsider 
 So much that you regret 
 
 There’s the one that’s just a knife 
 With a long sharp slicing blade 
 To cut away the bullshit 
 That one makes me most afraid 
 
 I’m so careful with that one 
 Like I said, I fear it most 
 I open it with caution
 It’s the weapon of the Holy Ghost
 
 There’s the one that can do damage
 That is like a little saw
 It goes this way first then that way
 As she lays down what’s the law
 
 That one uses confusion
 To keep the cutting process clean
 It creates a lot of pain
 While you wonder what she means
 
 Finally there’s the tweezers
 Of the questions that she asks
 To probe beneath the tissues
 For the problems they may mask
 
 All in all these tools are useful
 And produce more health than blood
 But when she says my hour’s over
 Even God can hear the thud
 
 Still, I’ll keep the whole set near me
 And use it when I can
 Cause there never was a toolsmith
 More beguiling to this man

Lyrics:

Alternating male and female verses where each shares their growth (his “it’s what that he does,” her “chart my own course”) that blend into shared harmonies on imagery like astronauts drifting and rescuing each other “face-to-face.” Mostly diatonic harmony with classic Broadway lifts and a key change or two near the end to heighten emotion. Arrangement builds steadily from reflective opening to a radiant, full ensemble feel on the final lines about being “part of each other’s story,” ending in a warm, joyful, curtain-call-ready cadence.

Title: Waitng for Love - Broadway Act One Finale
Lyrics:
[Intro]
 [arpeggiated piano, glockenspiel, legato strings]
 
 [Verse 1]
 [piano continues, soft strings]
 I guess I'm still waiting
 Hoping she'll come around
 I know I've been wishing
 I stood on more solid ground
 I thought maybe this time
 Things might turn out right
 So I wouldn't end up
 All alone in the night
 
 [Verse 2]
 At first someone might tell you
 How they've been waiting for love
 It's easy to believe them
 'Cause that's what you've been dreaming of
 You might start out believing
 You've finally opened the door
 To a heart that is worthy
 In someone you can adore
 
 [Chorus]
 But sooner or later
 So often it seems they won't leave that door open
 'Cause life has broken their dreams
 You can't figure out if it's them or it's you
 That can't keep love alive
 Despite the best you can do
 
 [Bridge]
 So you come back down
 To that place that you've known
 That feels so empty
 Yet sadly like home
 
 [Chorus 2]
 [timpani roll, strings swell]
 Yes I've been waiting for her
 Hoping she'll wake up and see
 She could leave heartache behind her
 If she'd only keep faith with me
 I still want to be with her
 I'd do whatever it takes
 But if she wants love only she
 Can keep her heart open
 And stop running away from its ache
 
 [Outro]
 [piano block chords, dramatic strings]
 It's not romance that sustains love
 There's a lot more that's involved
 It's the depths of one's soul
 It takes total resolve
 [vocal belt]
 I guess I'll just have to be glad
 That my heart's alive and still strives
 It'll never give up
 So I'll wait
 For what fate's got in store by and by
 [vocalizing]
 [final piano chord, cymbal swell]
Style Tags: Musical theater showtune featuring a baritone male vocal lead. The arrangement centers on a grand piano playing arpeggiated chords and rhythmic block patterns. A full orchestral string section provides legato counter-melodies and staccato accents. Orchestral percussion includes timpani rolls, crash cymbals, and a glockenspiel for melodic highlights. The piece features dramatic dynamic shifts, moving from intimate piano-vocal sections to a climactic, belt-style finale with soaring strings and brass-like synth textures. The tempo is approximately 75 BPM in 4/4 time, modulating through several keys for emotional lift.

Piano-led arrangement with warm but restrained strings and subtle acoustic guitar; minimal percussion, just a soft heartbeat-like pulse. Slow to moderate tempo, lyrical and flowing, with clear, singable melodies. Build gradually from sparse to slightly fuller near the final refrains and outro, but keep the overall sound intimate rather than showy. Mood: quietly devastated yet deeply devoted, conveying a man who cannot let go because her memory still feels like his light and home.

Title: We're All Going In Circles (Hip Hop)

 Steered my walk in her direction
 And gave me a poet’s eyes to see
 
 For my friend I sing this celebration

 It’s the fortress against life’s sorrows
 That only blesses a heart so pure

Title: Adam's Song
Lyrics:
There was a boy that I knew once - 
 When I was a much much younger man - 
 He hadn’t yet come of age - 
 When I was asked by a friend to give him a helping hand - 
 I worked as a lawyer then - 
 Before I had my family who've now grown - 
 And though this boy was young and no where near a man - 
 He had no living father of his very own -
 Adam was still so very green - 
 But though young, he had a stout and mighty heart - 
 He wouldn’t need a lot of help from me - 
 I could see that almost from the start -
 Way back in those early days - 
 Life for me was still mostly just a sport - 
 I ran rivers in the West - 
 When I wasn’t tied up working or in court -
 I taught Adam what I knew - 
 About how to master raging rivers and their ways - 
 And perhaps even a thing or two - 
 About what a man might one day have to brave -
 Adam quickly soaked it all right up - 
 And soon he shot far past me - 
 When it came to nerve and skill - 
 He proved the best I’d ever hope to see -
 My memory of our days on the water - 
 Of life on the river taken at the flood - 
 Are so warm from our adventures - 
 That it feels like Adam was my very own blood -
 As life went by we stayed in touch - 
 With what time we could both afford - 
 Soon Adam had a family of his own - 
 With two beloved daughters that he adored -
 It filled my heart to see him grow - 
 Into a devoted father and a man - 
 Who always gave his best to others - 
 Without expectations or demands -
 Alas, life took Adam far too soon - 
 He was cut down in his prime - 
 And I’ve lived to mourn that loss - 
 ‘Til now when I’m an old man making rhymes -
 These days I sit around writing songs - 
 About the passions of a life gone by - 
 But I remember Adam's spirit so well - 
 A first beloved son, though never really mine -
 My own sons and my daughters - 
 Along with Adam have been my life’s great prize - 
 And I can’t recall his smiling face - 
 Without tears filling up these old eyes -
 I only hope his darling girls - 
 Now grown into ladies I am sure -
 Hear this song and recognize their father’s heart - 
 Because there never was a heart more pure, -
 No there never was a heart more pure.

Title: Sunset On Ihlabela
Lyrics:
Como a minha, a mente da selva está sempre em outro lugar
 Verde infinitamente lentamente, sempre pacientemente
 As colinas densamente cobertas de Ilhabela
 Vegetação mais verde do que sua memória mais vívida
 Livre da nossa maldição necessária de pensar
 Com paciência infinita, a selva vive seus ciclos eternos
 Imperturbada por papagaios que pousam e depois pousam
 No ar mais rarefeito do que uma prece murmurada
 Macacos param momentaneamente em uma árvore para espiar
 Sem a menor pretensão de entender
 Curioso apenas sobre o que eu poderia fazer a seguir
 A luz do dia desaparece rapidamente
 Do outro lado da baía, luzes amarelas e brancas brilham na costa de San Sabastian
 Caminhando pelo píer, as pessoas reservam um tempo umas para as outras
 A água ondula em prata, azul e brilha em ouro
 O último dos nadadores de silhueta escura permanece
 Mantendo sua paz redentora
 Barcos artesanais de vermelho, branco, verde, amarelo e azul
 Booque calmamente no água
 Tendo servido hoje, como servirão amanhã
 Atendidos por gerações, subindo e descendo com a maré
 O roxo das montanhas se aprofunda
 À medida que os horizontes cor de água desaparecem
 A luz se derrama nas ruas de paralelepípedos
 Amantes, namorados e jazz suave de guitarras
 Familiar como o ar fresco da noite
 Flua sem pressa, enquanto as estrelas surgem acima
 De alguma forma, como a selva, eu pertenço aqui
 Parte deste todo orgânico

Piano- and strings-led orchestration with light rhythmic guitar and subtle percussion, giving gentle forward energy but not feeling like a big showstopper yet. Moderate, flowing tempo with a clear, singable hook on “And yet I love him” and “Without him / I know I would get by / But with him / Oh how the world comes alive.” Verses feel like an internal monologue about freedom, ambition, and identity, while the “I love him” sections bloom into more lyrical, romantic lines. Mostly diatonic harmony with a few modern Broadway lifts on key emotional phrases; no heavy jazz or pop-funk. Build from an introspective, lightly scored intro into a warmer, more expansive sound by the final “with him / oh how the world comes alive,” ending in a hopeful, open-feeling cadence that launches the story.

Title: All the kings horses (Publidhed)
Lyrics:
All the kings horses and all the Kings men
 Are so ineffective when there’s something to mend
 I know from experience for I took a fall
 Reliance on others did nothing at all
 When your shell is all broken, and your guts have spilled out
 Authority figures just don’t hold that much clout
 To reckon with Demons who escape through the cracks
 And turn back upon you, your soul to attack
 The King's men and horses don’t understand well
 Why they're useless for reconstructing a fragmented shell
 The shell of my ego was fractured to bits
 All my thoughts ran together, when I lost my wits
 Without its protection my fall scrambled my core
 I just wasn't the same as I had been before
 From the fall that I took something troubling awoke
 A deep-seated feeling that I ruptured my yoke
 A too-glib psychiatrist might put it like this
 Long before my shell fractured there was something amiss
 Exoskeletal reliance is now understood
 To reflect maladaption under the hood
 Still, I labored in solitude to restore my mind
 Seeing no future before me but only behind
 Still slowly time healed me, I regained my sight
 I began to see my way out of the night
 The shell that had broken only just when
 I really did not need it put together again

Title: Death on an Iceberg (Published)
Lyrics:
Last night I had another dream 
 And my dreams always come true
 It was about near death on an ice field
 And the path's I've walked through
 
 It started out in a bar 
 Of a fancy hotel 
 Where I'd gathered with acquaintances
 Who had nothing new to tell 
 
 From there somehow I got 
 To a hazy beach scene
 I was hanging out with some guys
 Who I’d known since our teens
 
 Familiar as they were
 They had nothing to say
 And before I knew it
 I had drifted away
 
 I wandered along 
 Distracted, feeling down
 Thinking thoughts that possessed me
 Not even looking around
 
 I wandered and wandered
 As through old feelings I combed
 When I finally looked back
 To see where I had roamed
 
 I must’ve been far north
 From where eskimos tramp in the snow
 Barely holding onto life
 Near fifty below
 
 The sun blazed in the sky
 Across the distance I'd walked
 The frozen sea that I'd covered
 Looked whiter than chalk
 
 I'd wandered so far
 The snow covered ice just merged with the sky
 I was numb to the cold
 But I feared I might die
 
 So I headed on back
 Till with people unknown
 I walked on unconnected
 Not feeling at home
 
 I guess the point of this dream
 And the point of this song
 Is slow death on a iceberg
 Comes when we no longer belong
 
 Don't put me out on an iceberg
 My true friends, please don't leave me alone
 Let's hold onto to one another
 Your all that I've known

Title: There Was Something About Her (Hip Hop V1)
Lyrics:
There was something about that girl 
 She could always figure out some sly way 
 When a night on the town was about over 
 For it to be somebody else’s turn to pay 
 
 She had a wink in her eye like the devil 
 She knew how to make every thing in life seem more fun 
 Than it ever would’ve been without her beside me
 But I felt like I was living life on the run 
 
 It never felt safe or felt solid to me
 But I guess I must’ve liked it that way
 Because I couldn’t say no to her antics
 And when I should’ve just left I would stay
 I let her abuse and neglect me
 I made up excuses for why she did that
 Until finally her selfish behavior
 Left her nowhere to turn so she showed me her back
 
 She took everything that she could manage
 And left in a huff as she walked out the door
 I’d like to think that I killed her with kindness
 But I guess her game just didn’t work anymore
 
 I’ve got to ask myself why I took it
 Looking back I know I didn’t act like a man

Title: Life Down At The Crossroads
Lyrics:
I’ve always hung out down at the Crossroads 
 No, it’s not that I lost my way 
 It’s just that down at the Crossroads 
 It feels like high noon at every time of day 
 
 I didn’t see myself as Wyatt Earp 
 Or any kind of Billy the Kid 
 I just needed to keep myself wide-awake 
 To play my hand and keep track of the bid
 
 Each road always offered me promise
 Promise that always came at a price 
 I watched other people choose one or another 
 Unlike me, unafraid to roll the dice 
 
 The reason that I’ve been stalled so long at the Crossroads 
 Just rattling the dice around in my hand 
 Was the adventure of the odds always changing 
 And I didn’t ever want to see those snake eyes land 
 
 Each road had its merits
 Dreams of good things further down the line, I’ll grant you that
 But once they chose one to commit to 
 They always started to see the world as a little bit more flat 
 
 I’m tired of shootouts at the Crossroads 
 Where my only aim was to hold my ground 
 I know it might have been better if I’d picked one 
 And moved on further along to settle down 
 
 But having spent my life at the Crossroads 
 And accepted that the Crossroads were my home 
 I look back and see that hanging out there 
 Is what allowed my troubled mind its treasured freedom to roam

Title: The Way Of Grace
Lyrics:
If there really are two ways of going in this world
 One by nature, one by Grace 
 Then I’ll abandon nature and go the other way 
 For what I’ve left to me and what I’ve yet to face 
 
 For most of my life I went the way of nature
 And I fought battles all along my way
 I tried to be a warrior as required
 By what seemed needed every day
 
 I put my hand to the plow by day
 And tried never to let up or look back
 But at night my dreams were always fevered
 And my sleep was fitful and nerve-racked
 
 The way of Grace obviously sounds much better 
 How could there ever have been any real choice
 Though for those who I chose to shepherd and to care for
 The way of nature seemed to have to fill my voice
 
 But now my labor’s are almost over
 It seems my job is just to make it to the end
 And so the way of Grace though so bare and lonely
 Appears to be my only life-long friend
 
 I have to accept that I can no longer change or affect much
 So describing beauty’s how I”ll use my voice
 I’ll try to leave behind a trail of words that may comfort
 Because the way of Grace is my final choice

Title: Death on an Iceberg (Published) (Remastered)
Lyrics:
Last night I had another dream 
 And my dreams always come true
 It was about near death on an ice field
 And the path's I've walked through
 
 It started out in a bar 
 Of a fancy hotel 
 Where I'd gathered with acquaintances
 Who had nothing new to tell 
 
 From there somehow I got 
 To a hazy beach scene
 I was hanging out with some guys
 Who I’d known since our teens
 
 Familiar as they were
 They had nothing to say
 And before I knew it
 I had drifted away
 
 I wandered along 
 Distracted, feeling down
 Thinking thoughts that possessed me
 Not even looking around
 
 I wandered and wandered
 As through old feelings I combed
 When I finally looked back
 To see where I had roamed
 
 I must’ve been far north
 From where eskimos tramp in the snow
 Barely holding onto life
 Near fifty below
 
 The sun blazed in the sky
 Across the distance I'd walked
 The frozen sea that I'd covered
 Looked whiter than chalk
 
 I'd wandered so far
 The snow covered ice just merged with the sky
 I was numb to the cold
 But I feared I might die
 
 So I headed on back
 Till with people unknown
 I walked on unconnected
 Not feeling at home
 
 I guess the point of this dream
 And the point of this song
 Is slow death on a iceberg
 Comes when we no longer belong
 
 Don't put me out on an iceberg
 My true friends, please don't leave me alone
 Let's hold onto to one another
 Your all that I've known

 We’d played all of our games, 
 Rhymed our favorite names

 There was still one thing more
 She knew yet lay in store
 To lift us all back up on our way
 As we came over that hill
 Atop of old Mayville
 We’d spot that lake come down from heaven where it lay
 
 Right there down the road 
 Sparkling just like gold 
 Shimmering bright in the sun 
 Joy soon returned 
 As our young hearts burned 
 To get out in that beauty she’d come from 
 
 That was her great gift
 For no matter how tired

 To give to the world
 As the long days unfurled
 From the gold in her heart at its core
 
 When she was gone
 It was hard to be strong
 That’s something we just couldn’t fake
 But we gathered once more
 Together by that shore
 And returned her forever to Heaven’s lake

 We’ll get by somehow
 If only for her memory’s sake
 She left us so much
 Her ever-warm touch
 And all the love left in her wake

Title: Afraid of Love (Country V2)
Lyrics:
I think maybe you’re afraid of love
 But that’s not something you would ever say
 It’s not that I’ve abused you
 Or demanded that you change your ways
 
 It was all really good to start with
 But the closer we got it seems
 I saw behind the mask you wear
 Into the place you stashed your dreams
 
 When I got close enough to feel them
 Maybe close enough to feel their life
 You backed off and shied away
 Like I’d come at you with a knife

 everybody wants someone else to care. 
 Deep down some may long to be worshipped, 
 But that's a setup for despair.
 
 Maybe love let them down before
 So they vow not to take a fall again, 
 but even so, their heart still longs 
 for their loneliness to end. 
 
 The real thing’s a different story. 
 It's not a fantasy of romance. 
 It's as simple as the earth we stand on, 
 Real feeling not some song and dance. 
 
 Everyone tells themselves they want love. 
 But real love won't let us hide 
 all the sorrow and the pain 
 that lingers deep inside. 
 
 No matter what people say, 
 you've got to watch for what they do. 
 They'll run away from real feeling
 And love that’s offered even when it’s true, 
 
 the truth is so many are not willing 
 to open up and risk more pain. 
 By letting someone inside deep enough
 to make them feel whole again
 
 You can see it everywhere
 In songs and movies that we play
 people denying their true feelings, 
 Still hoping love will happen anyway. 
 
 In the meantime, they put on armor 
 made of distance, and the thought 
 that they must protect their feelings, 
 And be careful lest they get caught. 
 
 Caught caring more than they should. 
 Caught being vulnerable. 
 Caught just being who they are. 
 Caught living life in full
 
 I’m offering you something precious
 No, not me, I’m no extraordinary man
 Just my open heart and honest feelings 
 And what life can mean, walking hand in hand

 I'm not asking for your past. 
 I'm just asking you to meet me

 It was all really good to start with

 I saw behind the mask you wear

 I'm not asking for your future.

Style Tags: Contemporary Broadway finale duet; older voices; joyful, heartfelt, and triumphant, with a sense of earned wisdom and shared destiny. Piano- and strings-led orchestration with warm brass swells and light percussion, giving forward energy without turning into bombastic pop-rock. Moderate, expansive tempo with clear, singable hooks on “now I know what it means for dreams to come true” and the “our dreams pulled us forward” sections. Alternating male and female verses where each shares their growth (his “it’s what that he does,” her “chart my own course”) that blend into shared harmonies on imagery like astronauts drifting and rescuing each other “face-to-face.” Mostly diatonic harmony with classic Broadway lifts and a key change or two near the end to heighten emotion. Arrangement builds steadily from reflective opening to a radiant, full ensemble feel on the final lines about being “part of each other’s story,” ending in a warm, joyful, curtain-call-ready cadence.

Title: The Hurricane
Lyrics:
What can you say about the power of nature
 When she decides to fight with all of her might
 Unleashing her terrible fury
 With Thunder and Lightning bolts setting fire to the Night
 
 Days before the hurricane hit
 Everything seemed peaceful and life still seemed safe
 Southern Appalachia had no idea 
 Of just what it was that was about to take place
 
 But when Helaine claimed her throne in the mountains
 Those that lived there fell out of grace 
 What folks had worked years for
 Quickly was turned into ruin and laid to waste
 
 In the mountain towns of the Carolinas
 And the hills of East Tennessee
 Ravaging winds and raging rivers
 Snapped poles like twigs and uprooted decades old trees
 
 Families trapped in the hurricane’s path
 Shuddered to hear its monstrous roar
 As windows shattered everywhere around them
 Falling trees pounded the ground
 Destroying homes that would house families no more. 
 
 From all over others responded
 Helping hands braved roads ravaged by floods
 Helicopters flew in to rescue those trapped by torrents of water
 And oceans of landsliding mud
 
 In old houses deep in the mountains
 Children prayed for deliverance from the storm
 The worst one seen in anyone’s lifetime
 By folks who lived there ever since they were born
 
 So many lives were soon lost
 So many were left without hope
 When Helaine took down their houses
 And rivers swept them away just like toy boats
 
 On a patch of high ground a few days later
 A lonely man sat and cried as he mourned
 Thinking of his lost ones as he remembered
 Holding his son on the day he was born
 
 Not everyone will remember
 The hell brought about by Helaine 
 But for many survivors and brave first responders
 It’s their shock and fear that will forever live in her name
 
 What can you say about the power of nature
 When she fights back with all of her might
 Unleashing her tremendous fury
 With Thunder and Lightning bolts That set fire to the Night
Style Tags: Contemporary Folk, raw, gritty, minor key, male voice, mournful

 Real feeling not some song and dance.

 people denying their true feelings, 
 Still hoping love will happen anyway.

Title: Afraid of Love

 people denying their true feelings,

Title: Ebb Tide
Lyrics:
There was a time when we were waves that rose together

 Each day became more slow and measured 
 I fell into a steady pace

 Now the tide has turned and it’s ebbing
 Time is pulling each one of us back
 It pooled us together at a party
 Where life’s waters calmed and slacked
 
 No one seemed diminished, though now we’re ripples
 Nowhere near the amplitude 
 That each one of us rose up to
 Whilst with youth we were embued
 
 But in the stillness of the tide pool 
 We were blessed to flow back into
 I could see beneath the surface
 What our waves’ motion once hid from view 
 
 You were all a part of the sea that swept me gasping
 Searching for a shore that really never was
 And though the tide’s now flowing ever backwards
 Clarifying waters is what time does
 
 I wish I’d seen you way back then
 As clearly as I see you now
 I might’ve known the treasure all around me
 And not been blinded by pursuit of useless petty crowns
